articles.title: HERE'S THE COMPLETE IPHONE 17 LINE-UP ACCORDING TO THREE HIGH-PROFILE LEAKERS
articles.summary: Three major leakers—Majin Bu, Fixed Focus Digital, and iIce Universe—have backed the same CAD drawing of the iPhone 17 lineup, which reveals three distinct camera layouts for the base, Air, and Pro models. While not an exact visual representation, the drawing highlights external element placements, hinting at a more complex camera design.

articles.title: GREEN PARTY'S CONTROVERSIAL NEW LOGO DESIGN HITS THE SPOT
articles.summary: Simple yet highly adaptable, the Green Party of Canada's new green dot logo symbolizes the planet and a circular economy. It seamlessly integrates into various brand icons representing key priorities like climate action and affordable housing. This minimalistic approach effectively reinforces the party's identity.

articles.title: BRIGHTLYLOOP LAUNCHES THE FIRST AI-DRIVEN MARKETPLACE FOR SCALABLE, INCLUSIVE DESIGN
articles.summary: BrightlyLoop, a women-owned AI marketplace, has launched an innovative platform to simplify UX, accessibility, and localization, helping businesses save millions by reducing reliance on costly consultants. Through its flagship tool, Brightly HeuristIQs, developed in partnership with George Mason University, businesses receive instant, culturally-adapted UX recommendations for efficient global expansion.

articles.title: DESIGNERS MUST GET BETTER AT UNCOMFORTABLE CONVERSATIONS
articles.summary: Designers must embrace difficult conversations to overcome industry challenges like sustainability, technological disruption, and diversity. By fostering curiosity, empathy, and the courage to ask uncomfortable questions, the design community can move beyond complacency. Avoiding these conversations only hinders progress and deepens existing issues.

articles.title: MULTI-FACTOR AUTHENTICATION DESIGN: SECURITY MEETS USABILITY IN UI/UX DESIGN
articles.summary: MFA (multi-factor authentication) enhances security while maintaining usability. This article explores various authentication methods, including passwords, 2FA, OTPs, biometrics, and passkeys. It provides best practices for designing secure, user-friendly authentication experiences, such as enforcing strong passwords, limiting login attempts, and educating users about security measures to improve overall UX.

articles.title: HUMAN-CENTERED DESIGN THROUGH AI-ASSISTED USABILITY TESTING: REALITY OR FICTION?
articles.summary: Unmoderated usability testing allows participants to complete tests independently, offering flexibility and broader reach. However, it lacks real-time moderator interaction. AI-driven follow-up questions aim to bridge this gap, but research shows limitations in generating informative, non-repetitive queries. While AI enhances qualitative insights, it risks frustrating participants with redundant or irrelevant prompts.

articles.title: PIXELATED CENSOR (FIGMA PLUGIN)
articles.summary: A simple Figma plugin that pixelates parts of designs to help designers hide sensitive information, redact text or images, or comply with NDAs.

articles.title: VISUALIZE ANYTHING USING 3D AND AI
articles.summary: Storyblocker utilizes a novel 3D-based solution to allow anyone to visualize their story anytime and anywhere. Take your idea from concept to final production utilizing AI and augmented reality technology.

articles.title: ACCESSIBLE COLOR PALETTE GENERATOR
articles.summary: Inspired by Radix UI's custom color palette tool, this color generation tool automates the creation of accessible enterprise UI color palettes.

articles.title: DESIGNING A SCALABLE AND ACCESSIBLE COLOR SYSTEM FOR YOUR DESIGN SYSTEM
articles.summary: A well-structured color system ensures consistency, scalability, and accessibility in design by using defined color scales, tokens, and best practices. Designers can create cohesive interfaces that work seamlessly across products and platforms by organizing colors into predictable shades and naming conventions.

articles.title: IN REGRETS ONLY'S CONSIDERED IDENTITY FOR MOMA MEMBERS, A PATH GUIDES BOTH THE EYE AND THE HEART
articles.summary: Regrets Only designed the MoMA Members identity, "Your MoMA," using a linear path to symbolize personal connections with art. Featuring MoMA Sans typography, dynamic alignments, and a nuanced color palette, the design balances individuality with MoMA's legacy.

articles.title: WHEN EVERYONE CAN BUILD ANYTHING
articles.summary: AI-driven tools make software development faster and more accessible, raising questions about the evolving role of quality, adoption, and design. As AI accelerates production, originality, taste, and craftsmanship become more valuable. The future of design lies in shaping AI inputs, ensuring high-quality outputs, and redefining strategy in a low-cost execution landscape.

articles.title: THIS MECHANICAL KEYBOARD WAS MILLED FROM A SINGLE BLOCK OF ALUMINUM
articles.summary: The Icebreaker is a Brutalist-style aluminum mechanical keyboard with a 65% layout, RGB lighting, aluminum keycaps, and dual dampening, priced from $1,500 wired and $1,600 wireless.

articles.title: NOSTALGIA IS POWERFUL – BUT DESIGNERS SHOULD BE WARY
articles.summary: Jaguar and GF Smith faced backlash for modern rebrands, showing how excessive change or reliance on the past can backfire.

articles.title: THIS ARTIST HAS PERFECTLY DESIGNED THESE AMAZING TATTOO TIGHTS
articles.summary: Gabby, an artist and designer, creates stunning printed tattoo tights, blending artistry with fashion.
